title: [RTL] Strength reduce and constant fold comparisons (Verilator lint)
body:
This not a BUG

As part of an effort to make emitted Verilog Lint clean.

The following FIRRTL programs,
compiled with `firtool --lower-to-rtl` produces Verilog,
`verilator --lint-only` reports the following Warning:

True for all comparison operators.

## %Warning-CMPCONST : Comparison is constant due to limited range

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    output tmp28: UInt<1>
    wire tmp11: UInt<1>
    tmp11 <= UInt<1>(1)
    tmp28 <= lt(tmp11, UInt<1>(1))
```

```verilog
module top_mod(
  output tmp28);

  wire tmp11;	// a_top_mod.fir:4:5

  assign tmp11 = 1'h1;	// a_top_mod.fir:5:{11,14}
  assign tmp28 = tmp11 < 1'h1;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3, :5:14, :6:14
endmodule
```

```verilog
%Warning-CMPCONST: a_top_mod_new.v:7:24: Comparison is constant due to limited range
                                       : ... In instance top_mod
    7 |   assign tmp28 = tmp11 < 1'h1;  
      |                        ^
                   ... Use "/* verilator lint_off CMPCONST */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```

## %Warning-UNSIGNED : Comparison is constant due to unsigned arithmetic

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input inp_0: SInt<11>
    output tmp12: UInt<1>
    output tmp2: UInt<1>
    wire tmp6: UInt<28>
    tmp2 <= geq(SInt<13>(0), inp_0)
    tmp6 <= mul(UInt<27>(1), tmp2)
    tmp12 <= gt(UInt<1>(0), tmp6)
```

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [10:0] inp_0,
  output        tmp12, tmp2);

  wire        _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:3:3
  wire [27:0] tmp6;	// a_top_mod.fir:7:5

  assign _T = $signed(13'h0) >= $signed({{2{inp_0[10]}}, inp_0});	// a_top_mod.fir:8:{10,13,17}
  assign tmp6 = {{27'd0}, _T} * 28'h1;	// a_top_mod.fir:9:{10,13}
  assign tmp12 = 28'h0 > tmp6;	// a_top_mod.fir:3:3, :10:14
  assign tmp2 = _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:3:3, :10:14
endmodule
```

```verilog
%Warning-UNSIGNED: a_top_mod_new.v:10:24: Comparison is constant due to unsigned arithmetic
                                        : ... In instance top_mod
   10 |   assign tmp12 = 28'h0 > tmp6;  
      |                        ^
                   ... Use "/* verilator lint_off UNSIGNED */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```
